title: leptaulus daphnoides
intro: Leptaulus daphnoides, also known as the wild rosemary or Thai rosemary, is a plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Thailand and other parts of Southeast Asia, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion. Leptaulus daphnoides is often prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is also used as a culinary herb in Thai cuisine.
